name: 3TC improved cognition and neuroinflammation markers in old mice
summary: 3TC provides the strongest aging-directed animal functional signal for reverse transcriptase inhibition in this track.
statement: In old wild-type mice, 3TC improved cognitive performance and reduced neuroinflammation and cytoplasmic DNA or innate-immune activation markers, with supporting evidence that transposable-element transcripts accumulate with human brain aging.
population_or_model: Old wild-type mice with supporting C. elegans and human brain transcriptome analyses
time_horizon: Six-week 3TC treatment in old mice in the main reported experiment
quantitative_note: Reported mouse cognitive testing used approximately 14 to 16 animals per group, with hippocampal RNA sequencing in 6 animals per group.
caveats: The functional intervention result is animal evidence.
Human transcriptome association does not prove human intervention benefit.
The mechanism is plausible but not fully isolated to LINE1 or a specific retrotransposon class.
